compiled_state.author: Iso Tester
compiled_state.description:
## Why
For an additive map the two signers must write distinct, self-identifying keys, and
completion validity needs a material fingerprint to detect post-signing edits.
## What
Both sign sites set an unsigned `data["kind"]` on the `SIGNATURE` event (`plan-review`,
`completion-verifier`) for robust routing (kind stays anchored via `manifest[0]`). The
completion signer ALSO records the plan-review material fingerprint
(description/AC/file_impact/children) as a manifest line, so completion validity-on-read can
recompute and compare (symmetric with plan-review). Drift-refresh re-sign preserves kind.
## Scope
`src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py` (`sign_plan_review`, `refresh_attestation`),
`src/rebar/_commands/transition.py` (`_verdict_manifest` + completion signing),
`src/rebar/signing.py` (kind passthrough if needed).
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Plan-review SIGNATURE events carry `data["kind"]=="plan-review"`.
- [ ] Completion SIGNATURE events carry `data["kind"]=="completion-verifier"`.
- [ ] Completion manifest records the material fingerprint (legacy completion sigs without it are treated as material-unknown by validity-on-read).
- [ ] `data["kind"]` and the fingerprint line do NOT alter the existing canonical payload in a way that breaks old signatures (no PAYLOAD_VERSION bump).
- [ ] Drift-refresh re-sign preserves the same kind.
## Added (review)
- The completion signer computes the material fingerprint via the SAME helper plan-review
  uses (single source of the material field set); it does not redefine the field list.

## Clarification
- `data["kind"]` is only a ROUTING HINT; the SIGNED `manifest[0]` remains authoritative (114f uses the hint only iff it matches the manifest-derived kind). This story just sets the hint consistently; it does not change what is signed.

## Clarification (review): data[kind] is consumed, not dropped
- `process_signature` (114f) consumes `data["kind"]` at REDUCE-TIME via its
  `attestation_kind(manifest, data)` routing (read directly from the event data, validated
  against the authoritative `manifest[0]`) — it is NOT silently dropped. Additionally, 114f
  stores the resolved/derived `kind` IN the signature record so the kind is self-describing
  for consumers (incl. the legacy `signature` mirror).
- [ ] The signers set `data["kind"]`; the reducer routes by it (validated against manifest[0]) and records the resolved kind in the signature record.
compiled_state.title: Signing call sites: stamp data[kind] on SIGNATURE events
